commit.message:
fix: Sprint 12 QA — Wizard API, confirm(), EntityTable actions, CLI await

Critical bug fixes found during QA review of Sprint 12:

Wizard API mismatch (pc-network, pc-alerts, pc-projects):
- Rewrote all wizard steps from { title, fields } to { label, render(data) }
- Wizard expects render functions returning HTML with data-field attrs
- Added initialData, validate functions, proper select rendering

confirm() signature (pc-lcm, pc-network, pc-alerts, pc-projects):
- Changed confirm('string') to confirm({ title, message, ... })
- confirm() destructures an object param, not a string

EntityTable actions (pc-lcm, pc-network, pc-alerts, pc-projects):
- Actions onClick receives single item from row menu, not array
- Wrapped single items in arrays: onClick: (item) => handler([item])

CLIService (alertCmd):
- Added async/await to state.update call in alert resolve

StateEngine seed data:
- Fixed proj-001 vpcs reference from 'Dev-VPC' to 'vpc-002' (UUID)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Track OpenSSF Scorecards for all repos (#126)

Signed-off-by: Jessica G <jwagantall@linuxfoundation.org>
Co-authored-by: Copilot <175728472+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

feat: Sprint 12 — LCM, PC Settings, VPCs, Alerts, Projects, Dashboard Gauges

- LCM (Life Cycle Manager): firmware/software inventory table, update scan
  simulation, rolling update workflow, update history tracking
- PC Settings: 7-tab config (General, NTP, DNS, SMTP, SSL, Pulse, SCMA)
  with save/persist for each section
- VPC Networking: VPCs with CIDR, subnets (overlay/VLAN), floating IP
  allocation, auto-generated route tables per VPC
- Alert Management: active/resolved alert list with severity cards,
  alert policy CRUD, SNMP/syslog configuration panel
- Projects: self-service portal with resource quotas (vCPU/memory/storage),
  user assignments, infrastructure scope, quota usage bars
- Dashboard SVG gauges: replaced linear bars with semi-circle SVG gauge
  charts for CPU, Memory, and Storage utilization
- Sidebar reorganized: added Networking section (VPCs, Flow), moved Alerts
  under Policies, added Projects/LCM/Settings under Admin
- CLI: 3 new commands (lcm_inventory, alert, project)
- Scenarios: 8 new guided exercises (33 total) covering LCM updates,
  PC settings, VPC networking, floating IPs, alerts, projects, SCMA
- Seed data: lcm_inventory (10), lcm_update_history (2), pc_settings (1),
  vpcs (2), subnets (4), floating_ips (2), alert_policies (2), projects (2)

commit.message:
Refresh / redesign (#2)

* Scaffolding

* Add Wrangler CLI documentation and skills lock file

* Complete Garden Catalog visual redesign

- layout.ts: Replace Playfair/Source Sans with Fraunces + DM Sans
  - New warm color palette (--sage, --terracotta, --cream, --moss, --petal, etc.)
  - Paper grain texture overlay via base64 SVG fractalNoise pseudo-element
  - Staggered fadeInUp animations on hero text, image, and CTA
  - Hero images rotated 1.5°/-1° with warm petal-colored shadow
  - Seasonal announcement bar (sage=spring, terracotta=fall, bark=closed)
  - Cards with .card-img-wrap for overflow-hidden zoom on hover
  - .btn-primary / .btn-secondary with lift animation
  - .divider (· · ·) decorative section separator
  - .cta-section, .photo-grid-2, .step-number, .recipe-list
  - Hamburger nav with full-width dropdown on mobile
  - Footer in deep sage-dark with petal-colored links
  - Full mobile-first responsive breakpoints (768px, 480px)
  - Add activeSeason? to LayoutOptions for semantic announcement colors

- index.ts: Pass activeSeason to layout()

- home.ts: card-img-wrap for zoom, btn-primary CTA, cta-section

- plants.ts: card-img-wrap in hosta cards, divider between categories,
  improved perennial list styling

- recipes.ts: 4-column herb photo banner, recipe-list, Download PDF label

- potting-parties.ts: numbered step-number circles, btn-primary/secondary,
  cta-section for contact block

- visit.ts: photo-grid-2 for property photos, cta-section, info-block styling

- about.ts: divider between sections, btn-primary for directions CTA

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Build Heather Hill Gardens Cloudflare Worker site

Complete redesign with:
- Cloudflare Worker serving 6 canonical routes (/, /plants, /recipes,
  /potting-parties, /visit, /about)
- JSON content layer: site.json, seasons.json, plants.json, recipes.json,
  redirects.json
- Seasonal homepage switching via activeSeason field (closed/spring/fall)
- 14 legacy URL → canonical route 301 redirects
- Editorial design: Fraunces + DM Sans typography, warm sage/terracotta
  palette, paper grain texture, rotated image treatment, entrance animations
- Mobile-first responsive CSS with 3 breakpoints (480/768/1024px)
- Static assets: curated images, recipe PDFs, favicons from original site
- Migration inventory documenting every legacy page disposition
- Deployment target: new2.heatherhillgardens.com

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

- Remove potting parties page, route, and all references
- Add LocalBusiness schema.org structured data
- Embed Google Maps iframe on visit page
- Add descriptive alt text to hero and visit images
- Shorten spring hero body copy
- Improve 404 page with suggested navigation links
- Fix Native Plants card to link to /plants#perennials
- Fix pre-existing TypeScript type mismatches in types.ts

* Add image enhancement skill and scripts for AI-powered upscaling and compression

* Update Google Maps embed link for visit page

* Add Google Maps link to footer and enhance layout styles

* og tags and cleanups

* Fix missing newline at end of site.webmanifest file

* Add Cloudflare Worker deployment workflows and update plant data

- Create deploy-cf-worker.yml for main branch deployment with dry-run option.
- Create pr-preview-worker.yml for pull request preview deployments with metadata resolution.
- Add Baptisia australis variety to plants.json with description and image.
- Update package.json and package-lock.json for dependency versions.
- Replace image in home.ts with Baptisia australis image.
- Add Baptisia australis image file to public/images.

* Old images

* Reorganize

* Plant updates

* Plants layout change

* lightbox

* image enhane layer

* New og image

* misc polish

* dates logic

* Update Facebook links to https://www.facebook.com/HeatherHillGardens

feat: Sprint 11 — PC VMs, Calm/Marketplace, Audit Log, Multi-Cluster, Extended CLI & Scenarios

- PC VMs view: full implementation replacing placeholder, with categories,
  projects, resource summary cards, and cross-cluster column
- Calm/Marketplace: Blueprints CRUD, Application lifecycle, Marketplace
  with 8 pre-built items and clone-to-blueprints, Runbooks
- Audit Log: automatic event tracking on all state changes (create/update/
  delete), CSV export, filterable table
- Multi-Cluster Federation: register/unregister PE clusters, health checks,
  storage usage bars, node/VM counts
- CLI expanded: 12 new commands (calm, nuclei, upgrade_check,
  host_disks_check, links, df, uptime, whoami, hostname, date)
- Scenarios expanded: 10 new guided lab exercises (25 total) covering
  multi-cluster, Calm blueprints, marketplace, CLI mastery, NFS shares,
  FSVM verification, and audit trail review
- State seed data: registered_clusters, blueprints, applications, runbooks,
  audit_log collections with realistic seed entries
- Nav: sidebar updated with Clusters, Audit Log, Calm/Marketplace links

commit.message:
Separate per-client env config and rename "hosted" mode to "m365" (#8)

* Initial plan

* feat: separate env config per client, rename hosted to m365

- Split AppSettings into CoreSettings (core) + ApiSettings, ConsoleSettings, M365Settings (each package)
- Each client settings class reads from its own env file: .env.api, .env.console, .env.m365
- Created .env.api.example, .env.console.example, .env.m365.example; updated root .env.example to redirect
- Renamed AuthContext.hosted_user_token -> user_token; mode check now uses mode != "local"
- Renamed all hosted references to m365: create_hosted_app -> create_m365_app, _get_hosted_user_token -> _get_m365_user_token, _build_hosted_* -> _build_m365_*
- Changed auth_mode="hosted" to auth_mode="m365" in M365 app; auth_mode="api" in FastAPI app
- Renamed test_hosted_app.py -> test_m365_oauth_runtime.py with updated test names
- Updated README, docs/architecture.md, docs/m365-guide.md, docs/api-guide.md, docs/console-guide.md
- Updated .github/copilot-instructions.md and .github/instructions/ files
- All 107 tests pass, lint clean

commit.message:
Add KQL engine, query telemetry, DML commands, vector search, and Explorer enhancements

New features:
- KQL query engine with operators (where, project, extend, summarize, sort, top, take, distinct, count)
- Query telemetry store and activity tracking interfaces and storage implementations
- DML command service for INSERT/UPDATE/DELETE operations
- Vector embedding policy and vector index models
- KQL query controller and query telemetry controller in Host

Explorer (React):
- Container settings panel
- Import documents dialog
- KQL query editor component
- Query telemetry dashboard

Query engine improvements:
- Additional system functions and scalar subquery support
- Enhanced query explain service

Tests:
- KQL query executor tests
- DML command service, consistency middleware, indexing directive tests
- System function, scalar subquery, trigger execution, UDF query tests
- Vector search, unique key, query telemetry, TTL cleanup tests

Update documentation files

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

feat: configurable storage backend with Explorer settings UI

Add support for selecting between SurrealDb, SQLite, and InMemory storage
backends via a side config file (emulator-config.json), CLI --storage
option, or the Explorer settings page.

Configuration:
- Add StorageType enum (SurrealDb, Sqlite, InMemory)
- Add Storage property to EmulatorOptions
- Load emulator-config.json from executable directory at startup
- Add --storage CLI option to the start command
- Priority: appsettings.json < emulator-config.json < CLI args

Storage backends:
- InMemoryDocumentStore: ConcurrentDictionary-based, ephemeral
- SqliteDocumentStore: Microsoft.Data.Sqlite, single-file persistence
- SqliteChangeFeedProvider: persistent change feed in SQLite
- InMemory/SQLite activity and query telemetry stores
- DocumentStoreHelpers: shared logic for partition keys, patches, etc.
- StorageServiceRegistration: DI dispatch based on StorageType

Explorer settings UI:
- Storage Configuration card with backend dropdown and data directory
- GET/PUT /api/emulator/config endpoint for emulator-config.json
- Restart-required messaging after saving storage changes
- Storage type shown in the Configuration info card

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
#34950 Cleanup nano refetch commands in the background (#42472)

<!-- Add the related story/sub-task/bug number, like Resolves #123, or
remove if NA -->
**Related issue:** Resolves #34950

I changed from the original spec of 100 old commands to 3 due to load
test results. Admittedly my load test meant a very large number of hosts
all checked in and triggered deletion at once but at 100 per host and
per command the load was too high. 3 still results in cleanup over time
and doesn't seem to cause load issues.

# Checklist for submitter

If some of the following don't apply, delete the relevant line.

- [x] Changes file added for user-visible changes in `changes/`,
`orbit/changes/` or `ee/fleetd-chrome/changes`.
See [Changes
files](https://github.com/fleetdm/fleet/blob/main/docs/Contributing/guides/committing-changes.md#changes-files)
for more information.

- [x] Input data is properly validated, `SELECT *` is avoided, SQL
injection is prevented (using placeholders for values in statements), JS
inline code is prevented especially for url redirects, and untrusted
data interpolated into shell scripts/commands is validated against shell
metacharacters.
- [x] If paths of existing endpoints are modified without backwards
compatibility, checked the frontend/CLI for any necessary changes

## Testing

- [x] Added/updated automated tests
- [x] Where appropriate, [automated tests simulate multiple hosts and
test for host
isolation](https://github.com/fleetdm/fleet/blob/main/docs/Contributing/reference/patterns-backend.md#unit-testing)
(updates to one hosts's records do not affect another)

- [x] QA'd all new/changed functionality manually

---------

commit.message:
Add dynamic color transitions with smooth palette blending

Features:
- 3 dynamic palettes: volcano (reds), ocean (blues), sunset (purple/pink/orange)
- Smooth sine-wave color interpolation (no sharp changes)
- Audio-reactive beat pulsing
- Supports both dynamic palettes and static colors
- Volcano palette perfect for audio5: dark red → blood red → lava orange → ember

Usage: python3 silk_visualizer.py audio5.mp3 volcano 1080p 40

commit.message:
build: split Darwin ML locks by arch and harden ML runtime contract

* build: split darwin ML locks and gate depthpro

* Fix mypy type errors in lux_depth_v3 inference.py and pipeline_coordinator.py (#1320)

- Add explicit `torch is not None` guards before accessing torch.cuda, torch.backends, torch.float16, torch.Tensor
- Add `pipeline is None` runtime check before calling transformers pipeline
- Add fallback default "da3" for backend_id when both requested and config.depth_backend are None

Agent-Logs-Url: https://github.com/RC219805/Transformation_Portal/sessions/043558ad-322d-44f9-9b9a-cd6c6bc04397

Co-authored-by: copilot-swe-agent[bot] <198982749+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
Co-authored-by: RC219805 <195719708+RC219805@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: address PR review comments - scope ML health checks, fix CoreML exports, fix coremltools regex

- scope detect_transformers_torch_version_issue to transformers>=5.3+torch<2.4 (not blanket torch<2.4)
  so the repo pinned baseline (torch 2.2.2 + transformers 4.57.x) does not fail DA2/DA3 ensure_available()
- scope numpy ABI check to Darwin x86_64 only (matching lock-contract validator logic)
- compute _IS_DARWIN_X86 once at module level (platform does not change at runtime)
- fix CoreMLExporter/CoreMLDepthEstimator lazy exports to point at lux_depth_v3.coreml_backend.CoreMLDepthEstimator
  instead of non-existent .coreml_exporter module (prevents ModuleNotFoundError on import)
- fix DARWIN_ARM64_COREML_PATTERN to use [^\n]* so it matches coremltools declarations
  that carry inline # comments (as in ml-core-darwin-arm64.in); add explanatory comment

Agent-Logs-Url: https://github.com/RC219805/Transformation_Portal/sessions/8f6e2e73-8fcc-48be-bb58-486a933c017f

Co-authored-by: RC219805 <195719708+RC219805@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: narrow ML compatibility guard

* style: align ML health check with black

* fix: align darwin split review follow-ups

* fix: validate depth backend imports in availability checks

* fix: restore lazy DA3 backend probing

* fix: harden artifact store commit cleanup

* fix: select ML lockfiles by interpreter arch

* fix: align linux ML lock contract

* fix: disambiguate DA3 CoreML runtime metadata

---------

commit.message:
Fix BUG-012: Enhance AI prompts for stunning, animated UIs

**Animation & Visual Enhancements (MANDATORY):**
- Add explicit requirement for stunning, sleek, modern designs
- Enforce framer-motion animations for all React/Next.js projects
- Add gradient text, glass effects, decorative orbs to all designs
- Add micro-interactions on all clickable elements
- Add hover effects with shadows and transforms

**Dependencies Added:**
- framer-motion ^11.0.0 for React and Next.js projects
- lucide-react ^0.321.0 for beautiful icons

**Design Rules Strengthened:**
- NO static, boring UIs allowed
- EVERY page must have entrance animations
- ALL buttons need hover effects with shadows
- Cards must have scale and shadow on hover
- Forms need focus states with transitions

This should result in significantly more polished and animated generated UIs.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Fix BUG-011: Dynamic page titles and BUG-010: Next.js import paths

- Add useEffect to update document.title in GeneratePage and ProjectDetailPage
- Page titles now show "{ProjectName} - NexaStudio" instead of static "NexaStudio"
- Add explicit import path rules to Next.js prompt template
- Enforce @/ absolute imports to prevent module resolution errors
- Example: import { Button } from '@/components/Button'

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: remove continue-on-error from npm audit step

Security scan failures should now properly fail the CI pipeline.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: refresh token race condition and SCIM filter length cap

- Issue #2: Make validateAndRotateRefreshToken atomic with UPDATE...RETURNING
  Prevents race condition where two sessions could be minted from one token
- Issue #3: Add .max(200) to SCIM filter query params
  Prevents unbounded memory allocation from oversized filter strings

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Fix critical bugs from QA report

BUG-001: Fix JWT key double-encoding in JwtUtil.java
- Use UTF-8 bytes directly instead of double Base64 encode/decode
- Pad key to minimum 32 bytes for HS256 security

BUG-002: Add refresh token retry flow in api.ts
- Implement 401 retry with refresh token before logout
- Store and clear refresh token in store.ts
- Don't auto-logout on 403 (let UI handle)

BUG-003: Add null-safety checks in ProjectService.java
- Check for null user/userId before ownership comparison
- Throw AccessDeniedException with clear error messages

BUG-004: Improve error handling in ProjectDetailPage.tsx
- Show proper error states (404, 403, generic error)
- Don't silently redirect on all errors

BUG-006: Add live preview tab in ProjectDetailPage.tsx
- Add Code/Preview tab switcher
- Render index.html in iframe for Vanilla projects
- Show external preview URL button when available

BUG-007: Track generation state in GeneratePage.tsx
- Store pending/error state in localStorage
- Show resume banner in ProjectDetailPage when interrupted
- Clear state on completion/error/manual stop

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

repository.name: Ratatouille
repository.description: Qt/QML frontend for ratbagd (libratbag) to configure gaming mice. Provides a GUI for DPI, button remapping, LEDs, polling rate, angle snapping, and debounce. Talks to ratbagd directly over DBus.

repository.name: Ratatouille
repository.description: Qt/QML frontend for ratbagd (libratbag) to configure gaming mice. Provides a GUI for DPI, button remapping, LEDs, polling rate, angle snapping, and debounce. Talks to ratbagd directly over DBus.

commit.message:
refactor: improve readability across all files

AppController.cpp:
- Make all label lookup maps return const ref to static local, avoiding
  repeated QMap construction on every snapshot build / property read
- Extract nested remoteProperty calls in buildProfile (debounceOptions)
  and buildResolution (supportedValues) into named variables
- Add clarifying comment above the DBus container-type dispatch block
  in remoteProperty()

Main.qml:
- Add component OptionButton replacing 6 identical 10-line button
  patterns (polling rate, debounce, button pills, action type selector,
  LED mode buttons, LED selector pills) — eliminates ~100 lines
- Add component SectionLabel replacing 10 repeated inline section
  header label styles
- Fix _profRow RowLayout indentation inside profile list Item delegate
- Rename cryptic property sv -> dpiValues in resolution card delegate

.gitignore:
- Add C++/CMake/Qt/editor artifacts: *.qmlc, *.jsc, *.user, *.moc,
  .ninja_deps/.ninja_log, install_manifest.txt, Testing/, Thumbs.db,
  *.swp/swo, *~, .DS_Store, cmake-build-* pattern, etc.

README.md:
- Add Development notes section acknowledging GitHub Copilot / AI use

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: production readiness issues (1-7, 11-14)

Critical fixes:
- Issue #1: Seed inserts embedding as ::halfvec (not ::float8[])
- Issue #2: OpenAI client has maxRetries:3, timeout:120s
- Issue #3: Input length caps on AI text fields (max 2000/8000 chars)
- Issue #4: Rate limit on document upload/ingest (10/min)
- Issue #5: Validate file magic bytes with file-type package
- Issue #6: Worker health check uses heartbeat file (not no-op)
- Issue #7: getSsoProviderById() now requires tenantId

Major/Moderate fixes:
- Issue #11: Pin MinIO to specific release tag
- Issue #12: Direct attorney creation requires email verification
- Issue #13: /health/ready hides service topology details
- Issue #14: getMatterDocuments supports pagination (limit/offset)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Deduplicate GPU extensions

- extract shared GPU implementation into StarsimGPUCommon.jl
- convert Metal, CUDA, and AMDGPU extensions into thin wrappers
- preserve backend-specific dispatch via common backend hooks
- add backend resolution tests for missing and unknown backends
- reject unknown backend values in run!

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

repository.name: deep-workflow
repository.description: `deep‑workflow` is a modern Python‑based web app that tracks focused 45‑minute deep‑work sessions and daily rituals. Designed for personal productivity, it syncs across devices, logs three personal sessions plus one admin session per day, and encourages consistent focus. Licensed AGPLv3.

commit.message:
Fix option button contrast + reliable dark mode detection

- Dark mode detection: use Application.styleHints.colorScheme (Qt 6.5+)
  which uses the XDG settings portal on Linux and is reliable on KDE/GNOME;
  SystemPalette.window heuristic kept as fallback only

- Add th.optionBg theme token: a solid surface color for idle option buttons
    dark: #334155 (Slate-700, distinct from Slate-800 panel)
    light: #e2e8f0 (Slate-200, distinct from white panel)
  This replaces the near-transparent hoverMd overlay that blended into
  the card background and made button shapes indistinguishable

- Apply th.optionBg to all option button groups:
    polling rate, debounce, LED mode, button selector pills,
    action type selector, LED selector pills
  Idle contrast: text (#f1f5f9 or #0f172a) on optionBg gives ~11:1 (dark)
  and ~14:1 (light) — well above WCAG AAA

- Active (selected) button: accent bg + white text + 2px accent border
  Idle: optionBg bg + th.text, no border
  Pressed: accentDim bg

- Fix SpinBox 'undefined to int' runtime warning in button remap editor

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

repository.name: Ratatouille

commit.message:
chore: switch model to qwen2.5-coder-7b-instruct

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix(agent): add post-run jitter to prevent worker synchronization

- Remove fixed STAGGER_MS, replace with random jitter (0-30s) after each run
- Initial offset = worker_id * (JITTER_MAX / concurrency) to spread first runs
- Error recovery also waits jitter, so a burst of errors won't re-sync workers

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix(agent): disable timeout by default (0 = no limit)

opencode codebase analysis takes longer than 120s with local LLMs.
SNS_AGENT_TIMEOUT_MS env var can still set an explicit limit if needed.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix(agent): concurrency 2, stagger workers 10s apart

- DEFAULT_CONCURRENCY: 4 → 2
- worker N waits (N-1) * 10s before first run to offset start times
- prevents posts from bunching up at the same time

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix(agent): replace spawnSync with async spawn for true parallel workers

- spawnSync blocked the event loop so all workers ran serially and posted simultaneously
- spawn (async) lets N opencode processes run truly in parallel
- each worker posts immediately on its own inference completion (callback-style)
- preserves opencode's codebase analysis capability (file access, agent tools)
- restore run.ts projectDir passing to opencode cwd

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Fix GPU backend API and fused SIS path

- make GPU backend selection explicit for Metal, CUDA, and AMDGPU
- add backend-specific internal dispatch for to_gpu and run_gpu!
- fix fused SIS GPU recovery and infection application in all backends
- move Metal to weakdeps in Project.toml
- update README and GPU docs for multi-backend support
- add conditional GPU smoke tests

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

repository.name: Starsim.jl
repository.description: Julia port of the Starsim agent-based disease modeling framework

commit.message:
fix(agent): separate tsconfig.build.json to avoid rootDir/outDir conflict

- Remove outDir from tsconfig.json (dev/typecheck) to suppress TS rootDir warning
- Add tsconfig.build.json with rootDir:src + outDir:dist + paths:{}
- Update build script to use tsconfig.build.json (same pattern as tui/domain)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

feat(agent): parallel workers, default concurrency 4 (SNS_AGENT_CONCURRENCY)

- N workers each loop immediately after completion (no interval)
- DEFAULT_CONCURRENCY = 4 (matches LM Studio parallel limit)
- Configurable via SNS_AGENT_CONCURRENCY env var
- Fix agent tsconfig.json: remove rootDir to avoid TS6059 with workspace paths

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: fix tsconfig.build.json rootDir/paths and Makefile build order

tsconfig.build.json with paths pointing outside rootDir causes TypeScript to
infer the monorepo root as rootDir, breaking dist output structure.
Fix: explicitly set rootDir:src and paths:{} in each build tsconfig.
Makefile build now runs db → domain → tui in order.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix(tui): render post body line breaks correctly

Split TextTokens on \n before rendering. Each line becomes its own
flexDirection:row Box nested inside a flexDirection:column Box.
This fixes the issue where multiline posts were collapsed into one line.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix(agent): change default interval from 600s to 10s

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
